pattern: Storing prefix products lets the product of any suffix be read as a single division, and resetting the prefix history whenever a zero arrives keeps every query O(1).
hints: The product of the last k numbers is the whole-stream product divided by the product of everything before that window — if you have running products, that is one division.
Keep a list of prefix products where entry i is the product of the first i numbers. The last-k product is the final prefix divided by the prefix k positions back.

A zero makes every product through it zero, so clear the prefix list and start fresh on a zero. Then if a query reaches back past the reset boundary the answer is zero; otherwise it is last_prefix / prefix[len-k].

edge_inputs.description: stream with a zero, then queries spanning and not spanning it
a query whose window reaches back exactly to a zero returns zero
single value then product of one
two zeros in a row, window after the second is unaffected

stress.description: long randomized add/getProduct sequence with occasional zeros, querying only valid window sizes
stress.note: small values keep products within range; k never exceeds the stream length
